briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To serve as the central orchestrator of the Dedoc document processing system, composing all specialized processors (converters, readers, extractors, constructors, attachment handlers) into a single, resilient parse pipeline with automatic MIME detection, error enrichment, and labeling mode support.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Document parsing orchestration, pipeline composition, error handling with metadata enrichment, MIME-based format auto-detection, labeling mode persistence. \par \b Input\/ \: File paths, optional processing parameters, configuration dictionaries. \par \b Output\/ \: ParsedDocument with content, structure, metadata, and attachments. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(9): dedoc.config, dedoc.manager_config; READS_DATA_FROM(8): filesystem; CALLS_COMPOSITION(9): ConverterComposition, ReaderComposition, StructureExtractorComposition, StructureConstructorComposition, MetadataExtractorComposition, AttachmentsHandler] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: Every parse() call returns a ParsedDocument or raises a DedocError with enriched metadata.
Pipeline steps execute in fixed order: convert → read → metadata → structure extract → output construct → attachments.
MIME fallback is attempted exactly once on ConversionError/BadFileFormatError. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why compose via dependency injection in init rather than hardcoding all components? A: Dedoc supports 17+ document formats each with specific readers and converters. DI allows testing with mock components and selective replacement of pipeline stages without touching orchestration logic. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Added SEMANTIC TEMPLATE markup and comprehensive LDD logging across all pipeline stages] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 10[Central document processing orchestrator] => DedocManager \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
